serviceOptions.seulOuCouple: Omra alone or as a couple
serviceOptions.famille: Omra as a family (3 to 7 people)

omraAccompagne.title: Accompanied Omra
omraAccompagne.description: Perform your Omra alongside qualified guides for reliable and caring accompaniment.
omraAccompagne.mainText: Perform your Omra with full confidence alongside our qualified guides, recognized for their seriousness, knowledge and ethics.
omraAccompagne.details: Present at every step, they accompany you with care so that you may experience this sacred journey in serenity, conformity with the rites and sincere pursuit of Allah's pleasure.

omraBadal.title: Omra Badal
omraBadal.shortDescription: A Mercy for your loved ones
omraBadal.description: Perform an Omra on behalf of a deceased or ill loved one, a pious act of immense spiritual value.
omraBadal.mainText: A journey of faith to the purest places on Earth, which erases sins and purifies the heart. From the Ihram to the Tawaf around the Kaaba, to the Sa'i between Safa and Marwa.
omraBadal.details: Congratulations on this magnificent journey of faith. You leave behind the worries of this world to reach the honored Makkah, the blessed land that Allah has magnified.

visiteGuidee.title: Guided Tours
visiteGuidee.description: Explore the holy sites of Medina and Makkah with expert guides to understand prophetic history.
visiteGuidee.mainText: Visiting the historical sites of Makkah and Medina connects you to the life of the Prophet ﷺ and his companions.
visiteGuidee.details: Visit Mount Uhud, the mosque of Quba, and the historical sites of the Battle of the Trench. A total immersion in the history of Islam.

hadiths.title: The sacredness (Ḥaram) of Makkah
hadiths.arabic: إِنَّ هَذَا الْبَلَدَ حَرَّمَهُ اللَّهُ يَوْمَ خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ، فَهُوَ حَرَامٌ بِحُرْمَةِ اللَّهِ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ
hadiths.translation: Allah made this city sacred on the day He created the heavens and the earth. It remains sacred by Allah’s decree until the Day of Resurrection.

hadiths.title: Prohibition of shedding blood and cutting trees in Makkah
hadiths.arabic: لَا يَحِلُّ لِامْرِئٍ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ أَنْ يَسْفِكَ فِيهَا دَمًا، وَلَا يَعْضُدَ بِهَا شَجَرًا
hadiths.translation: It is not lawful for one who believes in Allah and the Last Day to shed blood there or cut down its trees.

hadiths.title: Makkah, the most beloved land to Allah
hadiths.arabic: وَاللَّهِ إِنَّكِ لَخَيْرُ أَرْضِ اللَّهِ، وَأَحَبُّ أَرْضِ اللَّهِ إِلَى اللَّهِ، وَلَوْلَا أَنِّي أُخْرِجْتُ مِنْكِ مَا خَرَجْتُ
hadiths.translation: By Allah, you are the best land of Allah and the most beloved land to Allah. Had I not been expelled from you, I would never have left you.
hadiths.source: Sunan at-Tirmidhi, 3925 (Hasan Sahih, authenticated by al-Albani)

hadiths.title: The reward of prayer in al-Masjid al-Ḥarām
hadiths.arabic: صَلَاةٌ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ خَيْرٌ مِنْ مِائَةِ أَلْفِ صَلَاةٍ فِيمَا سِوَاهُ
hadiths.translation: One prayer in al-Masjid al-Haram is better than one hundred thousand prayers elsewhere.
hadiths.source: Musnad Ahmad, 14694 (authenticated by al-Albani in Sahih al-Jami‘)

hadiths.title: The Kaaba, the first house established on earth
Allah's protection of Makkah
hadiths.arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ حَبَسَ عَنْ مَكَّةَ الْفِيلَ، وَسَلَّطَ عَلَيْهَا رَسُولَهُ وَالْمُؤْمِنِينَ
hadiths.translation: Allah held back the elephant from Makkah, then granted it to His Messenger and the believers.

hadiths.title: Angels guard the gates of Makkah
hadiths.arabic: عَلَى أَنْقَابِ مَكَّةَ مَلَائِكَةٌ، لَا يَدْخُلُهَا الطَّاعُونُ وَلَا الدَّجَّالُ
hadiths.translation: At the mountain passes of Makkah there are angels. Neither plague nor the Dajjal will enter it.

hadiths.title: The gravity of sinning in Makkah
hadiths.arabic: مَنْ أَحْدَثَ فِيهَا حَدَثًا أَوْ آوَى مُحْدِثًا فَعَلَيْهِ لَعْنَةُ اللَّهِ
hadiths.translation: Whoever introduces an offense there or shelters an offender is under the curse of Allah.

hadiths.title: The Prophet's ﷺ supplication for Makkah
hadiths.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ حَبِّبْ إِلَيْنَا مَكَّةَ كَحُبِّنَا الْمَدِينَةَ أَوْ أَشَدَّ
hadiths.translation: O Allah, make Makkah beloved to us as we love Madinah, or even more.

hadiths.title: Medina is a sacred sanctuary (Haram)
hadiths.arabic: إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَرَّمَ مَكَّةَ، وَإِنِّي حَرَّمْتُ الْمَدِينَةَ، مَا بَيْنَ لَابَتَيْهَا، لَا يُقْطَعُ شَجَرُهَا، وَلَا يُحْدَثُ فِيهَا حَدَثٌ
hadiths.translation: Ibrahim made Makkah sacred, and I have made Madinah sacred between its two lava plains. Its trees are not to be cut and no grave offense is to be committed there.

hadiths.title: Medina is sacred between its two lava fields
hadiths.arabic: إِنِّي أُحَرِّمُ مَا بَيْنَ لَابَتَيِ الْمَدِينَةِ
hadiths.translation: I declare sacred what lies between the two lava plains of Madinah.

hadiths.title: The curse upon those who desecrate Medina
hadiths.arabic: مَنْ أَحْدَثَ فِيهَا حَدَثًا أَوْ آوَى مُحْدِثًا فَعَلَيْهِ لَعْنَةُ اللَّهِ وَالْمَلَائِكَةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ
hadiths.translation: Whoever introduces an offense there or shelters an offender will be under the curse of Allah, the angels, and all people.

hadiths.title: Medina expels the wicked like a bellows
hadiths.arabic: إِنَّ الْمَدِينَةَ كَالْكِيرِ، تَنْفِي خَبَثَهَا وَيَنْصَعُ طَيِّبُهَا
hadiths.translation: Madinah is like a furnace: it expels its impurities and leaves its pure part clear.

hadiths.title: Faith will retreat to Medina
hadiths.arabic: إِنَّ الْإِيمَانَ لَيَأْرِزُ إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ كَمَا تَأْرِزُ الْحَيَّةُ إِلَى جُحْرِهَا
hadiths.translation: Faith will retreat to Madinah just as a snake retreats to its hole.

hadiths.title: The Prophet's ﷺ supplication for Medina's blessings
hadiths.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِي مَدِينَتِنَا، وَفِي صَاعِنَا، وَفِي مُدِّنَا، وَزِدْ مَعَ الْبَرَكَةِ بَرَكَةً
hadiths.translation: O Allah, bless our Madinah, bless our sa‘ and our mudd, and add blessing upon blessing.

hadiths.title: Encouragement to die in Medina
hadiths.arabic: مَنْ اسْتَطَاعَ مِنْكُمْ أَنْ يَمُوتَ بِالْمَدِينَةِ فَلْيَمُتْ بِهَا، فَإِنِّي أَشْفَعُ لِمَنْ يَمُوتُ بِهَا
hadiths.translation: Whoever among you can die in Madinah, let him die there, for I will intercede for the one who dies there.
hadiths.source: Musnad Ahmad, 16645 (authenticated by al-Albani, Sahih at-Targhib 1196)

hadiths.title: Patience with Medina's hardships
hadiths.arabic: لَا يَصْبِرُ عَلَى لَأْوَائِهَا وَشِدَّتِهَا أَحَدٌ إِلَّا كُنْتُ لَهُ شَهِيدًا أَوْ شَفِيعًا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ
hadiths.translation: No one patiently endures its hardship and difficulty except that I will be a witness or an intercessor for him on the Day of Resurrection.

omraHadith: The Prophet ﷺ said: "The Omra expiates sins committed between it and the previous one."
medinaHadith: A prayer in my mosque is better than a thousand prayers elsewhere, except the Sacred Mosque.

legal.title: Legal notices
legal.cgvuSubtitle: General terms of sale and use
